{{Quote|Worst of ''all'', that contact makes ''certain'' what we could only ''suspect'' up to now-- ''Darth Vader is alive--''!|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="Star Wars (1977) 21">[[Star Wars (1977) 21|''Star Wars'' (1977) 21]]</ref>}}
'''''Star Wars 21: Shadow of a Dark Lord''''' is the twenty-first issue in the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] series of comics.

==Plot summary==
[[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]] escapes from [[Simon Greyshade/Legends|Simon Greyshade's]] suite, where she was held captive, and finds [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], who has now recovered from his trance-like state. Luke reveals that his trance was caused by touching someone else's mind through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] for a moment; someone strong with the Force who he suspects to be [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. At the same time Greyshade is meeting with his new partner [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Zertik Strom/Legends|Strom]], [[The Wheel/Legends|The Wheel's]] administrator who has secretly sent his [[Wheel Security Force]] to subdue Strom's troops and recover the money that they had stolen from The Wheel. And now that [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] is getting ready to enter the main event of [[The Big Game]] he realizes that [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] is also entered in the contest, and according to the rules there can be only one survivor. Meanwhile on the planet [[Ultaar/Legends|Ultaar]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] finds the remains of a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] information retrieval team wiped out by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Beilert Valance/Legends|Beilert Valance]]. Vader realizes that Valance is searching for the same thing as himself: the boy who destroyed the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star I]]. The race is on to see who finds him first.

==Continuity==
This issue marks the first appearance of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] in the series since the adaptation of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. It also foreshadows the telepathic bond between Luke and Vader, which will be greatly expanded upon in [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']].
